name: Jersey City Police
description_short: Jersey City Police Federal Credit Union is a member-owned credit union established in 1950, serving government employees with savings accounts, auto loans, and personal banking services.
description_long:
Jersey City Police Federal Credit Union was established in 1950 and is headquartered in Jersey City, New Jersey. As a federally chartered credit union, it operates as a not-for-profit financial institution with a focus on serving public sector workers, particularly law enforcement personnel and government employees. The credit union has grown to serve over 1,000 loyal members and stewards approximately $8.8 million in assets.

The credit union offers a range of financial products and services including regular savings accounts, share draft (checking) accounts, money market accounts, share certificates (CDs), auto loans (both new and used vehicles), personal loans, mortgage options, and online bill pay services. Members have access to competitive interest rates and low fees as benefits of the not-for-profit structure. The institution provides online banking and mobile apps for iOS and Android devices, allowing members to manage accounts remotely.

Jersey City Police FCU distinguishes itself through its membership eligibility focused on government employees and public sector workers, offering tailored financial services designed for this demographic. The credit union operates with a single branch location at 161 Sterling Avenue in Jersey City and maintains direct customer service through phone support at (201) 547-5388. Their routing number is 221276163, and they employ over 2 dedicated staff members as of 2026.

As a smaller credit union with $8.8 million in assets and 1,000+ members, Jersey City Police FCU serves a niche market of government employees but may have limited branch access and ATM networks compared to larger institutions. The organization currently has its lobby closed, though online banking services remain operational. While they offer competitive rates on auto loans and personal lending products, potential members should verify membership eligibility requirements and understand the limitations of a single-branch operation. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
pros: Established credit union with 74+ years of history and stability since 1950
Competitive auto loan rates (4.21% for new vehicles, used vehicle loans available)
Not-for-profit structure offers better rates and lower fees than for-profit banks
Online banking and mobile apps available for iOS and Android
Mortgage loan options available in addition to personal and auto loans
Dedicated to serving government employees and public sector workers
Online bill pay services included for members
cons: Only 1 branch location with 0 ATMs, severely limiting accessibility for members outside Jersey City
Lobby currently closed, requiring members to use online services or phone banking
Small institution with $8.8M in assets may limit product offerings compared to larger credit unions
Membership restricted to government employees, excluding general public
No reviews available on the site, making it difficult to assess member satisfaction
best_for: Jersey City-based government and law enforcement employees seeking member-owned banking
Public sector workers looking for competitive auto and personal loan rates
Government employees wanting to support a community-focused not-for-profit financial institution
diagnosis: Jersey City Police Federal Credit Union is best suited for government and law enforcement employees in the Jersey City area seeking member-owned banking with competitive loan rates. The main caveat is the single-branch operation with no ATMs and membership eligibility restrictions that exclude the general public, making it inaccessible for most consumers.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
